Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space. 90: - **File modified**: [App.tsx](file:///Users/sahilpatel/Desktop/New_Mantra_puja/Store/src/App.tsx) 91: - **Concept**: Fully connected the devotee-facing storefront to fetch, merge, and display published Pooja products dynamically from the Supabase database. 92: - **Key Features**: 93: 1. **Supabase Client Fetching**: Added a dynamic `loadPublishedProducts` function in `App.tsx` importing the verified `supabase` client. It runs a query to fetch all published items: `.from('website_pooja_products').select('*').eq('is_published', true)`. 94: 2. **Consolidated Prop Mapping**: Structured the database records on load to match the TSX `Product` and `PoojaProduct` interfaces exactly, converting types (prices, ratings) safely. 95: 3. **Conflict-Free Catalog Merging**: Prepend dynamically published database products to the storefront list while using a Set of IDs to filter out any identical mock cards. This ensures that custom pooja products created in the admin panel are showcased first without duplication. 96: 4. **Highly Reactive Navigation Sync**: Added a reactive `useEffect` that triggers `loadPublishedProducts()` on component mount and on every page transition (whenever the active page changes). This guarantees that whenever an administrator publishes drafts, the storefront cards update instantly in real-time as soon as the devotee browses the shop, categories, search, or details. 97: 5. **High-Range Price Filtering**: Discovered that premium Pujas and Havans (costing over $100, such as the created product at $151) were in ximum limit of $100 on the storefront price slider. Re-engineered `ShopPage.tsx` to extend the range limit to $1000, ensuring all premium and customized Pooja offerings are instantly visible to devotees under the default catalog search layout. 98: 6. **Dynamic Image-URL Rendering**: Solved layout breakage where the raw Cloudflare R2 image URL was printed directly on the page inside a large `` tag. Added conditional checking across all devotee-facing pages (including `ShopPage.tsx`, `CategoryPage.tsx`, `SearchPage.tsx`, `WishlistPage.tsx`, `UserProfilePage.tsx`, `ProductModal.tsx`, and `ProductDetailPage.tsx`'s recommended related items). If a product's image matches a standard web URL, the application renders a styled `` tag with clean centering and `objectFit: 'cover'`; otherwise, it smoothly falls back to the default spiritual emoji view. 99: 7. **Visual Modal Flex-Stretch Fix**: Resolved CSS flexbox layout constraints in the Quickview popover ([ProductModal.tsx](file:///Users/sahilpatel/Desktop/New_Mantra_puja/Store/src/components/ProductModal.tsx#L88-L115)) where taller right-hand details stretched the left column but left specified-height images at their original height, displaying an empty grey background below the graphic. Re-engineered the left column container to use `minHeight: '350px'` instead of a fixed height, coupled with `alignItems: 'stretch'`, enabling the binary image layout to expand dynamically and cover the complete stretched vertical area of the card. 100: 101: --- 102: 103: ## 🔬 Compilation & Build Status 104: 105: The application has been verified through strict production compiler checks: 106: - **Build Command**: `npx tsc --noEmit` 107: - **Compiler Output**: Clean validation of all TSX structure, props, types, and hooks, with **zero compilation or type errors**. 108: 109: --- 110: The above content does NOT show the entire file contents.
